chunks.text: Stereo. H.C. JD A 38. Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, BAHAWALPUR BENCH, BAHAWALPUR. JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 (The State Vs. Allah Rakha) Criminal Appeal No. 408 of 2021 (Allah Rakha and another Vs. The State and another.) J U D G M E N T Date of hearing: 28.11.2022. Appellants by: Mr. Muhammad Umair Mohsin, Advocate. State by: Ch. Asghar Ali Gill, Deputy Prosecutor General. Complainant by: Mr. Saleem Abbas, Advocate. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J.–– The convicts namely Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen and Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas were tried along with Tariq and Allah Ditta, their co- accused, both since acquitted, by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Fortabbas in case F.I.R. No.25 of 2021 dated 28.01.2021 registered at Police Station Marrot, District Bahawalnagar in respect of offences under sections 302, 452, 324, 337-F(vi), 337-L, 34 and 109 P.P.C. for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Mst. Noor Fatima daughter of Muhammad Saleem (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 30.09.2021, convicted Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen and Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas (convicts) and sentenced them as infra: Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 2 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen. i) Death under section 302(b) P.P.C. as Tazir for committing Qatl- i-Amd of Mst Noor Fatima daughter of Muhammad Saleem (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.500,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased, in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment.

The learned trial court vide judgment dated 30.09.2021, convicted Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen and Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas (convicts) and sentenced them as infra: Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 2 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen. i) Death under section 302(b) P.P.C. as Tazir for committing Qatl- i-Amd of Mst Noor Fatima daughter of Muhammad Saleem (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.500,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased, in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. ii) Rigorous Imprisonment for ten years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1) and directed to pay fine of Rs. 50,000/- and in default thereof to undergo further simple imprisonment for six months. iii) Pay Daman of Rs.50,000/- under section 337-L P.P.C. to Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1) and in default thereof to be kept in jail till the payment of the same and the period of imprisonment due to failure to pay Daman be considered as simple imprisonment. iv) Rigorous Imprisonment for ten years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Haram Fatima and directed to pay fine of Rs. 50,000/- and in default thereof to further six months of simple imprisonment. v) Pay Daman of Rs.1,00,000/- under section 337-F(vi) P.P.C. to Haram Fatima and in default thereof to be kept in jail till the payment of the same and the period of imprisonment due to failure to pay Daman be considered as simple imprisonment. vi) Rigorous Imprisonment for seven years under section 452 P.P.C. and directed to pay fine of Rs. 50,000/- and in default thereof to further six months of simple imprisonment. Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas. i) Imprisonment for life under section 302(b) P.P.C. as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Mst Noor Fatima daughter of Muhammad Saleem (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.400,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased, in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. ii) Rigorous Imprisonment for seven years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1) and directed to pay fine of Rs. 30,000/- and in default thereof to undergo further simple imprisonment for six months. iii) Rigorous Imprisonment for seven years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Haram Fatima and

The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. ii) Rigorous Imprisonment for ten years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1) and directed to pay fine of Rs. 50,000/- and in default thereof to undergo further simple imprisonment for six months. iii) Pay Daman of Rs.50,000/- under section 337-L P.P.C. to Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1) and in default thereof to be kept in jail till the payment of the same and the period of imprisonment due to failure to pay Daman be considered as simple imprisonment. iv) Rigorous Imprisonment for ten years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Haram Fatima and directed to pay fine of Rs. 50,000/- and in default thereof to further six months of simple imprisonment. v) Pay Daman of Rs.1,00,000/- under section 337-F(vi) P.P.C. to Haram Fatima and in default thereof to be kept in jail till the payment of the same and the period of imprisonment due to failure to pay Daman be considered as simple imprisonment. vi) Rigorous Imprisonment for seven years under section 452 P.P.C. and directed to pay fine of Rs. 50,000/- and in default thereof to further six months of simple imprisonment. Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas. i) Imprisonment for life under section 302(b) P.P.C. as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Mst Noor Fatima daughter of Muhammad Saleem (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.400,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased, in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. ii) Rigorous Imprisonment for seven years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1) and directed to pay fine of Rs. 30,000/- and in default thereof to undergo further simple imprisonment for six months. iii) Rigorous Imprisonment for seven years under section 324 P.P.C. for attempting to commit Qatl-i-Amd of Haram Fatima and Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 3 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 directed to pay fine of Rs. 30,000/- and in default thereof to further six months of simple imprisonment. All the sentences awarded to the convict namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas were ordered to run concurrently by the learned trial court and the benefit provided under section 382-B of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898, was also extended to the convict namely Muhammad Ramzan by the learned trial court. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen and Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas (convicts) lodged Criminal Appeal No.408 of 2021 assailing their conviction and sentences. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.14 of 2021 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentence of death awarded to the appellant Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen. We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal 408 of 2021 and Murder Reference No.14 of 2021 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the facts necessary, as disclosed through the statement of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), the complainant of the case, are as under:- “Stated that Mst.Sumaira Bibi solemnized marriage with me against the wishes of her brothers and she is owner of land measuring 4/5 kanals. My wife promised to return said land to her brothers. On 28.01.2021 at about 11:00 am, accused Allah Rakha S/o Muhammad Yasin, Muhammad Ramzan S/o Ilyas came by riding on 125 motorcycle and entered into my house. I made hue and cry, witnesses Muhammad Asghar S/o Wali Muhammad and Shah Rasool S/o Shair Muhammad attracted at the spot. Meanwhile, accused Allah Rakha made fire shot with pistol which hit on my left arm, while he made second fire shot on my left buttock which went through and through.

I made hue and cry, witnesses Muhammad Asghar S/o Wali Muhammad and Shah Rasool S/o Shair Muhammad attracted at the spot. Meanwhile, accused Allah Rakha made fire shot with pistol which hit on my left arm, while he made second fire shot on my left buttock which went through and through. I fell down and I saw that my niece Noor Fatima, who was present in the court yard of my house, accused Allah Rakha made fire shot at my niece Noor Fatima which hit on her right thigh. Accused Allah Rakha again made fire shot on my niece Haram Fatima which hit on her right lower leg. PWs Asghar and Shah Rasool tried to stop the accused Allah Rakha but he made fire shot at P.W.s but they escaped. Then accused persons fled away by riding on motorcycle along with pistol. Then P.W.s took us to Hospital Marrot. Police came at R.H.C Marrot and recorded my statement Exh.P.A, upon which I affixed my thumb impression and signatures Exh.P.A/1. Then, I along with my nieces Haram Fatima Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 4 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 and Noor Fatima were referred to BVH Bahawalpur. On the same night at about 1 1/2/02:00 a.m i.e. 29.01.2021, my niece Noor Fatima succumbed to the injuries. Accused Allah Rakha and Muhammad Ramzan committed the occurrence on the abetment of Liaqat S/o Khurasheed, Tariq S/o Shoukat and Allah Ditta S/o Yasin.” 4. After the formal investigation of the case report under section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 was submitted before the learned trial court and the accused were sent to face trial. The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 04.05.2021, to which the appellants pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. (PW-13) for their onward transmission to the office of the Collection Centre, Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Multan.

(Carbon copy present on file has been shown to her and she has identified her writing, signatures and stamp.)” The prosecution also got Dr. Wasif (PW-11) examined, who on 28.01.2021, was posted as Medical Officer at R.H.C. Marrot and on the same day medically examined Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1). Dr. Wasif (PW-11), after examining Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), observed as under:-: “INJURY NO.1- According to opinion of BVH Bahawalpur, a lacerated wound measuring about 5x2cm on posterior surface of left forearms about 7cm below left elbow joint. Adjacent area had redness but there was no scar mark, bruise or burning. There was no cutting of muscle, easy to moveable, bone crepitus not present. INJURY NO.2. Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 8 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 According to opinion of BVH, Bahawalpur surgeon, there was 1x1em penetrating wound (entry wound) on abdomen. The margins were irregular & inverted. No burning or tatooing present. The wound was 2cm blow & lateral to left iliac crest. The adjacent area have no bruise, laceration or scar mark. According to opinion of BVH, Bahawalpur surgeon, there was about 2x2cm larger wound (exit wound) on medial and upper quadrant of left hip and 4 inches away from anus. The wound was rounded with everted margins and irregular. No burning or tatooing present. The adjacent area have no bruise, laceration or scar mark. Fresh blood was oozing from wound. OPINION: Injury No.1 was declared other hurt U/S. 337-L(ii) PPC while injuries No.2&3 were firearm injuries. Probable duration of injuries was 2 to 4 hours.

OPINION: Injury No.1 was declared other hurt U/S. 337-L(ii) PPC while injuries No.2&3 were firearm injuries. Probable duration of injuries was 2 to 4 hours. Possibility of fabrication, No.” 7. On 10.08.2021 the learned Assistant District Public Prosecutor gave up the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Saleem, Muhammad Latif, Shah Muhammad and Matloob Ahmad 891/C as being unnecessary. On 16.09.2021, the learned Assistant District Public Prosecutor closed the prosecution evidence after tendering the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore regarding the blood stained earth (ExPBB) and the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh. P.A.A.) regarding the analysis of the pistol recovered from the appellant namely Allah Rakha and the empty shells of the bullet collected from the place of occurrence. Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 9 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellants namely Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen and Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to question why this case against you and why the P.W.s have deposed against you, the appellants replied that the case was false and the witnesses had deposed untruthfully. The appellants namely Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen and Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas opted not to get themselves examined under section 340 Cr.P.C and did adduce any evidence in their defence. 9. At the conclusion of the trial, the learned trial court convicted and sentenced the appellants as referred to above. 10. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellants precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and that the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible and relevant evidence.

At the conclusion of the trial, the learned trial court convicted and sentenced the appellants as referred to above. 10. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellants precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and that the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible and relevant evidence. The learned counsel for the appellants in support of this appeal, further contended that the story of the prosecution mentioned in the F.I.R., on the face of it, was highly improbable and the reason assigned by the complainant and the eye-witnesses for being present at the place of occurrence was without any justification. He further contended that the statements of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellants further submitted that the recoveries were full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value and result of fake proceedings. He finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of doubt. Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 10 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 11. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond the shadow of doubt by producing independent witnesses. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further argued that the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellants. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3). The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, further argued that the recovery of the pistol from the appellant namely Allah Rakha and the report of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.

The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3). The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, further argued that the recovery of the pistol from the appellant namely Allah Rakha and the report of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh. P.A.A.) also corroborated the ocular account. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offenders with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, prayed for the rejection of the appeal. 12. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellants, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant and with their assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial. Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 11 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 13. In the first instance, we would like to discuss the prosecution case with regard to the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas. We have observed that the case of the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas is distinct from the case of the appellant namely Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen. According to the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW- 2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3), the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas had arrived at the place of occurrence along with his co-convict namely Allah Rakha (appellant) on a motorcycle, however, all the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) did not mention in their statements before the learned trial court that the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan actually entered the premises of the house where the occurrence took place.

We have observed that the case of the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas is distinct from the case of the appellant namely Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen. According to the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW- 2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3), the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas had arrived at the place of occurrence along with his co-convict namely Allah Rakha (appellant) on a motorcycle, however, all the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) did not mention in their statements before the learned trial court that the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan actually entered the premises of the house where the occurrence took place. Furthermore, all the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) stated that they had seen the appellant namely Allah Rakha, armed with a pistol, firing at the deceased as well as the injured witness, however, did not state that the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan was even armed with any weapon or that if he even deboarded from the motorcycle which he was allegedly driving at the time of occurrence. It is also not discernible from the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW- 1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) that the co-accused of the appellant namely Allah Rakha (the other appellant) needed any help of the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan in committing the murder of the deceased or causing injuries to the witnesses. We have also noted that during the course of investigation, Sikandar Hayat, A.S.I. (PW-13), the Investigating Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 12 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 Officer of the case, did not observe any trail marks of the wheels of the motorcycle which was allegedly being driven by the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan at the time of occurrence. We have also noted that according to the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh. P.H.) as prepared by Muhammad Din Abid, draftsman (PW-5) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh. P.U.) as prepared by Sikandar Hayat, A.S.I. (PW-13), the presence of the appellant namely Allah Rakha has been mentioned as point No.1 in the said site plans, whereas the presence of the motorcycle, which motorcycle the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan was driving at the time of occurrence, has been shown as point No.6 in the site plan having a distance of as many as 110 feet from each other.

P.H.) as prepared by Muhammad Din Abid, draftsman (PW-5) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh. P.U.) as prepared by Sikandar Hayat, A.S.I. (PW-13), the presence of the appellant namely Allah Rakha has been mentioned as point No.1 in the said site plans, whereas the presence of the motorcycle, which motorcycle the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan was driving at the time of occurrence, has been shown as point No.6 in the site plan having a distance of as many as 110 feet from each other. In this manner, the prosecution could not prove that the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan was even in knowledge of what the other appellant namely Allah Rakha was doing at the time of occurrence, when, allegedly the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan was present as far as 110 feet away on a motorcycle. The most important fact proving the failure of the prosecution to prove the presence of the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan, at the place of occurrence, at the time of the occurrence, remains the evidence of the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) who all never stated that the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan either accompanied the other appellant namely Allah Rakha to the place of occurrence or that the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan even deboarded from the motorcycle. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General has also argued that on 14.02.2021, the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan led to the recovery of the motorcycle, hence, the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 13 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) are supported sufficiently. The recovery of the motorcycle from the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan cannot be relied upon as the Investigating Officer of the case did not join any witness of the locality during the said recovery, which action of his was in clear violation of the provisions of the section 103 Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 and therefore the evidence of the recovery of the motorcycle cannot be used as incriminating evidence against the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan, being evidence which was obtained through illegal means and hence hit by the exclusionary rule of evidence.

Appeal No.408 of 2021 (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) are supported sufficiently. The recovery of the motorcycle from the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan cannot be relied upon as the Investigating Officer of the case did not join any witness of the locality during the said recovery, which action of his was in clear violation of the provisions of the section 103 Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 and therefore the evidence of the recovery of the motorcycle cannot be used as incriminating evidence against the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan, being evidence which was obtained through illegal means and hence hit by the exclusionary rule of evidence. The provisions of section 103 Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898, unfortunately, are honoured more in disuse than compliance. To appreciate it better, this section is being reproduced:- "103.-- Before making a search. under this chapter, the officer or other person about to make it shall call upon two or more respectable inhabitants of the locality in which the place to be searched is situate to attend and witness the search and may issue an order in writing to them or any of them so to do. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Muhammad Ismail and others Vs. The State” ( 2017 SCMR 898) at page 901 has held as under:- “For the above mentioned recovery of weapons the prosecution had failed to associate any independent witness of the locality and, thus, the mandatory provisions of section 103, Cr.P.C. had flagrantly been violated in that regard.” We have also noted that the prosecution witnesses namely Manzoor Ahmad (PW-1), Shah Rasool (PW-2) and Muhammad Asghar (PW-3) failed to Murder Reference No. 14 of 2021 14 Crl. Appeal No.408 of 2021 establish any connection of the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan with the motive due to which the occurrence had taken place. For the reasons mentioned above, we are of the opinion that the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas has a distinguishable case from the other appellant namely Allah Rakha son of Muhammad Yaseen. Considering all the above circumstances, we entertain serious doubt in our minds regarding the involvement of the appellant namely Muhammad Ramzan son of Muhammad Ilyas in the present case.
